records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Regarding the Execution of Retaliation (qisas)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5370 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Aban Al-Ahmari, who narrated from Abu Basir Yahya ibn Abi Al-Qasim Al-Asadi, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"When the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) was nearing his death, Jibril (as) descended and said:
'O' Messenger of Allah (swt), would you like to return to the world?'
Prophet (sw) replied: 'No, I have conveyed the messages of my Lord (azj).'
Jibril (as) repeated the offer.
Prophet (sw) responded: 'No, rather [I choose] the Highest Companion.'
Then the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), while the Muslims were gathered around him, said: 'O' people, there is no prophet after me and no tradition after my tradition. Whoever claims otherwise after this, his claim and innovation are in the Fire, so kill him and whoever follows him, for they are in the Fire.
O' people, revive retribution (qisas) and uphold the right for its rightful owner. Do not become divided. Submit to Allah (swt) and be at peace so that you may be safe.'
Allah (swt) has decreed:
"Surely I will prevail, I and My messengers." Indeed, Allah (swt) is All-Powerful, Almighty."
(Surah Al-Mujadilah 58:21)
records.arabicText:
5370 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبَانٍ اَلْأَحْمَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي اَلْقَاسِمِ اَلْأَسَدِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "لَمَّا حَضَرَتِ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْوَفَاةُ نَزَلَ جَبْرَئِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ هَلْ لَكَ فِي اَلرُّجُوعِ إِلَى اَلدُّنْيَا فَقَالَ "لاَ قَدْ بَلَّغْتُ رِسَالاَتِ رَبِّي" فَأَعَادَهَا عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "لاَ بَلِ اَلرَّفِيقَ اَلْأَعْلَى" ثُمَّ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ اَلْمُسْلِمُونَ حَوْلَهُ مُجْتَمِعُونَ "أَيُّهَا اَلنَّاسُ إِنَّهُ لاَ نَبِيَّ بَعْدِي وَ لاَ سُنَّةَ بَعْدَ سُنَّتِي، فَمَنِ اِدَّعَى بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَدَعْوَاهُ وَ بِدْعَتُهُ فِي اَلنَّارِ فَاقْتُلُوهُ وَ مَنِ اِتَّبَعَهُ فَإِنَّهُ فِي اَلنَّارِ أَيُّهَا اَلنَّاسُ أَحْيُوا اَلْقِصَاصَ وَ أَحْيُوا اَلْحَقَّ لِصَاحِبِ اَلْحَقِّ وَ لاَ تَفَرَّقُوا أَسْلِمُوا وَ سَلِّمُوا تَسْلَمُوا
۝ كَتَبَ اَللّٰهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَ رُسُلِي إِنَّ اَللّٰهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ ۝ "
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5370 - Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Aban Al-Ahmari, who narrated from Abu Basir Yahya ibn Abi Al-Qasim Al-Asadi, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) was nearing his death, Jibril (as) descended and said:
'O' Messenger of Allah (swt), would you like to return to the world?'
Prophet (sw) replied: 'No, I have conveyed the messages of my Lord (azj).'
Jibril (as) repeated the offer.
Prophet (sw) responded: 'No, rather [I choose] the Highest Companion.'
Then the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), while the Muslims were gathered around him, said: 'O' people, there is no prophet after me and no tradition after my tradition. Whoever claims otherwise after this, his claim and innovation are in the Fire, so kill him and whoever follows him, for they are in the Fire.
O' people, revive retribution (qisas) and uphold the right for its rightful owner. Do not become divided. Submit to Allah (swt) and be at peace so that you may be safe.'
Allah (swt) has decreed:
"Surely I will prevail, I and My messengers." Indeed, Allah (swt) is All-Powerful, Almighty."
(Surah Al-Mujadilah 58:21)

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Thief Who Overpowers a Woman for Her Private Part and Kills Her Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5371 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a thief who entered upon a woman to steal her belongings. After gathering the clothes, his desires overcame him, and he violated her. When her son stirred, the thief struck him with an axe he had with him and killed him. After finishing, the thief carried the clothes and was about to leave when the woman attacked him with the axe and killed him. The thief's family came the next day demanding retaliation for his blood.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "His guardians who sought retaliation for his blood must pay the blood money for the boy. The thief is liable for what he left behind - four thousand dirhams - for having forced himself upon her because he committed adultery. This amount must be taken from his wealth. As for the woman, she bears no responsibility for killing him because he was a thief."
records.arabicText: 5371 - رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ ع قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ سَارِقٍ دَخَلَ عَلَى اِمْرَأَةٍ لِيَسْرِقَ مَتَاعَهَا فَلَمَّا جَمَعَ اَلثِّيَابَ تَبِعَتْهَا نَفْسُهُ فَوَاقَعَهَا فَتَحَرَّكَ اِبْنُهَا فَقَامَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَتَلَهُ بِفَأْسٍ كَانَ مَعَهُ فَلَمَّا فَرَغَ حَمَلَ اَلثِّيَابَ وَ ذَهَبَ لِيَخْرُجَ حَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ بِالْفَأْسِ فَقَتَلَتْهُ فَجَاءَ أَهْلُهُ يَطْلُبُونَ بِدَمِهِ مِنَ اَلْغَدِ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ "يَضْمَنُ مَوَالِيهِ اَلَّذِينَ طَلَبُوا بِدَمِهِ دِيَةَ اَلْغُلاَمِ وَ يَضْمَنُ اَلسَّارِقُ فِيمَا تَرَكَ أَرْبَعَةَ آلاَفِ دِرْهَمٍ بِمَا كَابَرَهَا عَلَى فَرْجِهَا لِأَنَّهُ زَانٍ وَ هُوَ فِي مَالِهِ يَغْرَمُهُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا فِي قَتْلِهَا إِيَّاهُ شَيْءٌ لِأَنَّهُ سَارِقٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5371 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a thief who entered upon a woman to steal her belongings. After gathering the clothes, his desires overcame him, and he violated her. When her son stirred, the thief struck him with an axe he had with him and killed him. After finishing, the thief carried the clothes and was about to leave when the woman attacked him with the axe and killed him. The thief's family came the next day demanding retaliation for his blood.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "His guardians who sought retaliation for his blood must pay the blood money for the boy. The thief is liable for what he left behind - four thousand dirhams - for having forced himself upon her because he committed adultery. This amount must be taken from his wealth. As for the woman, she bears no responsibility for killing him because he was a thief."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Thief Who Overpowers a Woman for Her Private Part and Kills Her Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5372 - Muhammad ibn Al-Fudayl narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman and killed the child in her womb. The woman then took a knife and stabbed him, killing him.
Imam (as) said: "The blood of the thief is void (without consequence)."
records.arabicText: 5372 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ لِصٍّ دَخَلَ عَلَى اِمْرَأَةٍ وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى فَقَتَلَ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا فَعَمَدَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِلَى سِكِّينٍ فَوَجَأَتْهُ بِهِ فَقَتَلَتْهُ قَالَ "هَدَرَ دَمُ اَللِّصِّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5372 - Muhammad ibn Al-Fudayl narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman and killed the child in her womb. The woman then took a knife and stabbed him, killing him.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood of the thief is void (without consequence)."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Thief Who Overpowers a Woman for Her Private Part and Kills Her Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5373 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say regarding a man who tried to seduce a woman for unlawful relations, and she threw a stone at him, striking him in a fatal spot and killing him.
Imam (as) said: "There is nothing upon her in the matter between her and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And if she is brought before a just Imam, he will declare his blood to be void."
records.arabicText: 5373 - وَ رَوَى الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع يَقُولُ‌ فِي رَجُلٍ رَاوَدَ امْرَأَةً عَلَى نَفْسِهَا حَرَاماً فَرَمَتْهُ بِحَجَرٍ فَأَصَابَتْ مِنْهُ مَقْتَلًا قَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا شَيْ‌ءٌ فِيمَا بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَإِنْ قُدِّمَتْ إِلَى إِمَامٍ عَدْلٍ أَهْدَرَ دَمَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5373 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say regarding a man who tried to seduce a woman for unlawful relations, and she threw a stone at him, striking him in a fatal spot and killing him.
Imam (as) said: "There is nothing upon her in the matter between her and Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And if she is brought before a just Imam, he will declare his blood to be void."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Thief Who Overpowers a Woman for Her Private Part and Kills Her Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5374 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Zurara, who said:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who forces himself upon a woman.
Imam (as) said: "He is to be killed."
records.arabicText: 5374 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَغْصِبُ اَلْمَرْأَةَ نَفْسَهَا قَالَ "يُقْتَلُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5374 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Zurara, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who forces himself upon a woman.
Imam (as) said: "He is to be killed."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Who Brings a Man Into Her husband's House, and Her Husband Kills Him, and the Woman Then Kills Her Husband, and What is Obligatory in That Case
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5375 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who married a woman, and on the night of consummation, the woman brought in a male friend of hers and let him enter the bridal chamber. When the husband approached to be intimate with his wife, the friend suddenly attacked him, and they fought in the house until the husband killed the friend. Then the woman struck the husband and killed him because of her friend.
Imam (as) said: "The woman must pay the blood money for her (male) friend, and she is to be executed for killing her husband."
records.arabicText: 5375 - رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ تَزَوَّجَ اِمْرَأَةً فَلَمَّا كَانَ لَيْلَةُ اَلْبِنَاءِ عَمَدَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ إِلَى رَجُلٍ صَدِيقٍ لَهَا فَأَدْخَلَتْهُ اَلْحَجَلَةَ فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ اَلرَّجُلُ يُبَاضِعُ أَهْلَهُ ثَارَ اَلصَّدِيقُ فَاقْتَتَلاَ فِي اَلْبَيْتِ فَقَتَلَ اَلزَّوْجُ اَلصَّدِيقَ وَ قَامَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فَضَرَبَتِ اَلرَّجُلَ ضَرْبَةً فَقَتَلَتْهُ بِالصَّدِيقِ قَالَ "تَضْمَنُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ دِيَةَ اَلصَّدِيقِ وَ تُقْتَلُ بِالزَّوْجِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5375 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who married a woman, and on the night of consummation, the woman brought in a male friend of hers and let him enter the bridal chamber. When the husband approached to be intimate with his wife, the friend suddenly attacked him, and they fought in the house until the husband killed the friend. Then the woman struck the husband and killed him because of her friend.
Imam (as) said: "
records.thaqalaynMatn: The woman must pay the blood money for her (male) friend, and she is to be executed for killing her husband."

records.chapter: Chapter on One Who Dies in the Crowds of Eid, Arafah, or Near a Well or Bridge, and it is Unknown Who Killed Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5376 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Whoever dies in the crowd of Friday prayer, Eid, Arafah, at a well, or on a bridge, and it is unknown who caused his death, his blood money is to be paid from the Public Treasury (Bayt Al-Mal)."
records.arabicText: 5376 - رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ مَاتَ فِي زِحَامِ جُمُعَةٍ أَوْ عِيدٍ أَوْ عَرَفَةَ أَوْ عَلَى بِئْرٍ أَوْ جِسْرٍ لاَ يُعْلَمُ مَنْ قَتَلَهُ فَدِيَتُهُ عَلَى بَيْتِ اَلْمَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5376 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever dies in the crowd of Friday prayer, Eid, Arafah, at a well, or on a bridge, and it is unknown who caused his death, his blood money is to be paid from the Public Treasury (Bayt Al-Mal)."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who is Killed and Found in Dismembered Parts
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5377 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Al-Fadl ibn Uthman, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who was killed, and his head was found in one tribe, his torso and arms in another tribe, and the rest of his body in yet another tribe.
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon the tribe where his torso and arms were found, and the funeral prayer is to be performed over him."
records.arabicText: 5377 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنِ اَلْفَضْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُقْتَلُ فَيُوجَدُ رَأْسُهُ فِي قَبِيلَةٍ وَ وَسَطُهُ وَ صَدْرُهُ وَ يَدَاهُ فِي قَبِيلَةٍ وَ اَلْبَاقِي فِي قَبِيلَةٍ قَالَ "دِيَتُهُ عَلَى مَنْ وُجِدَ فِي قَبِيلَتِهِ صَدْرُهُ وَ يَدَاهُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5377 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Al-Fadl ibn Uthman, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who was killed, and his head was found in one tribe, his torso and arms in another tribe, and the rest of his body in yet another tribe.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon the tribe where his torso and arms were found, and the funeral prayer is to be performed over him."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who is Killed and Found in Dismembered Parts
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5378 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who was killed and whose body parts were found scattered.
How should the funeral prayer be performed for him?
Imam (as) said: "The prayer should be performed over the part that contains his heart."
records.arabicText: 5378 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ قُتِلَ وَ وُجِدَ أَعْضَاؤُهُ مُتَفَرِّقَةً كَيْفَ يُصَلَّى عَلَيْهِ قَالَ "يُصَلَّى عَلَى اَلَّذِي فِيهِ قَلْبُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5378 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about a man who was killed and whose body parts were found scattered.
How should the funeral prayer be performed for him?
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prayer should be performed over the part that contains his heart."

records.chapter: Chapter on Head Wounds and Their Names
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Al-Asmaʿi states:
The first type of head injury (shijaj) is the hariṣah, which is the one that scratches or splits the skin. From this comes the saying, "The launderer tore (haraṣa) the cloth," meaning he ripped it.
Then comes the baḍiah, which is the injury that cuts through the flesh after the skin.
Next is the mutalahimah, which penetrates into the flesh but does not reach the thin membrane covering the bone (simhaq).
Then is the simhaq, which is the wound where only a thin layer remains between it and the bone. Every thin layer is called simhaq. This term is also used to describe thin clouds in the sky (samaʾ samahiq) and thin layers of fat on a sheep.
Following that is the muḍihah, which exposes and reveals the bone.
Then comes the hashimah, which crushes the bone.
After that is the munaqqilah, which shatters the bone into pieces, causing fragments (farash Al-ʿiẓam) - thin layers that remain on the bone beneath the flesh. The poet Al-Nabighah referenced this in his verse: "And following them are the thin fragments of their brows."
Then is the maʾmumah, which reaches the protective covering of the head (umm Al-raʾs), the membrane covering the brain.
Among other types of wounds are the jaʾifah, which penetrates into the internal body cavity or, in the case of the head, reaches the brain.
records.arabicText: قَالَ الْأَصْمَعِيُّ أَوَّلُ الشِّجَاجِ الْحَارِصَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَحْرِصُ الْجِلْدَ يَعْنِي تُشَقِّقُهُ وَ مِنْهُ قِيلَ حَرَصَ الْقَصَّارُ الثَّوْبَ أَيْ شَقَّهُ ثُمَّ الْبَاضِعَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَشُقُّ اللَّحْمَ بَعْدَ الْجِلْدِ ثُمَّ الْمُتَلَاحِمَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي أَخَذَتْ فِي اللَّحْمِ وَ لَمْ تَبْلُغِ السِّمْحَاقَ ثُمَّ السِّمْحَاقُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي بَيْنَهَا وَ بَيْنَ الْعَظْمِ قِشْرَةٌ رَقِيقَةٌ وَ كُلُّ قِشْرَةٍ رَقِيقَةٍ فَهِيَ سِمْحَاقٌ وَ مِنْهُ قِيلَ فِي السَّمَاءِ سَمَاحِيقُ مِنْ غَيْمٍ وَ عَلَى الشَّاةِ سَمَاحِقُ مِنْ شَحْمٍ‌ ثُمَّ الْمُوضِحَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تُبْدِي وَضَحَ الْعَظْمِ ثُمَّ الْهَاشِمَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَهْشِمُ الْعَظْمَ ثُمَّ الْمُنَقِّلَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا فَرَاشُ الْعِظَامِ وَ فَرَاشُ الْعِظَامِ قِشْرَةٌ تَكُونُ عَلَى الْعَظْمِ دُونَ اللَّحْمِ وَ مِنْهُ قَوْلُ النَّابِغَةِ وَ يَتْبَعُهُمْ مِنْهَا فَرَاشُ الْحَوَاجِبِ‌ ثُمَّ الْمَأْمُومَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَبْلُغُ أُمَّ الرَّأْسِ وَ هِيَ الْجِلْدَةُ الَّتِي تَكُونُ عَلَى الدِّمَاغِ وَ مِنَ الشِّجَاجِ وَ الْجِرَاحَاتِ الْجَائِفَةُ وَ هِيَ الَّتِي تَبْلُغُ فِي الْجَسَدِ الْجَوْفَ وَ فِي الرَّأْسِ الدِّمَاغَ.

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Kills and Then Flees
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5379 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Zharif ibn Nasih, from Aban ibn Uthman, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who intentionally killed another man and then fled, and he could not be captured until he died.
Imam (as) said: "If he had wealth, the blood money is taken from it. If he had no wealth, it is taken from his closest relatives, and then from those next closest to him."
records.arabicText: 5379 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ ظَرِيفِ بْنِ نَاصِحٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ رَجُلاً عَمْداً ثُمَّ فَرَّ فَلَمْ يُقْدَرْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى مَاتَ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ مَالٌ أُخِذَ مِنْهُ وَ إِلاَّ أُخِذَ مِنَ اَلْأَقْرَبِ فَالْأَقْرَبِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5379 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Zharif ibn Nasih, from Aban ibn Uthman, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who intentionally killed another man and then fled, and he could not be captured until he died.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he had wealth, the blood money is taken from it. If he had no wealth, it is taken from his closest relatives, and then from those next closest to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding One Who Kills and Then Flees
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5380 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Ibn Bukayr, from Ubayd ibn Zurara, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who was apprehended with multiple legal punishments (hudud) upon him, one of which was execution.
Imam (as) said: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to carry out the other hudud first and then execute him. Do not oppose Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as)."
records.arabicText: 5380 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنِ اِبْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يُؤْخَذُ وَ عَلَيْهِ حُدُودٌ إِحْدَاهُنَّ اَلْقَتْلُ قَالَ "كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُقِيمُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحُدُودَ قَبْلُ ثُمَّ يَقْتُلُهُ وَ لاَ تُخَالِفْ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5380 - Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Ibn Bukayr, from Ubayd ibn Zurara, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who was apprehended with multiple legal punishments (hudud) upon him, one of which was execution.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to carry out the other hudud first and then execute him. Do not oppose Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5381 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad Al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"For an Al-muḍihah (a wound that exposes the bone), the compensation is five camels.
For the as-simhaq (a wound just below the skin but not exposing the bone), it is four camels.
For the Al-munaqqilah (a wound that causes bone fragments), compensation is fifteen camels.
For the Al-jaʾifah (a wound that reaches the internal organs), it is one-third of the full blood money, which is thirty-three camels.
Likewise, for the Al-maʾmumah (a wound reaching the membrane of the brain), it is also one-third of the full blood money."
records.arabicText: 5381 - رَوَى اَلْقَاسِمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ اَلْجَوْهَرِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "فِي اَلْمُوضِحَةِ خَمْسَةٌ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ وَ فِي اَلسِّمْحَاقِ اَلَّتِي دُونَ اَلْمُوضِحَةِ أَرْبَعَةٌ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ وَ فِي اَلْمُنَقِّلَةِ خَمْسَةَ عَشَرَ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ وَ فِي اَلْجَائِفَةِ ثُلُثُ اَلدِّيَةِ ثَلاَثٌ وَ ثَلاَثُونَ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ وَ فِي اَلْمَأْمُومَةِ ثُلُثُ اَلدِّيَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5381 - Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad Al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"For an Al-muḍihah (a wound that exposes the bone), the compensation is five camels.
For the as-simhaq (a wound just below the skin but not exposing the bone), it is four camels.
For the Al-munaqqilah (a wound that causes bone fragments), compensation is fifteen camels.
For the Al-jaʾifah (a wound that reaches the internal organs), it is one-third of the full blood money, which is thirty-three camels.
Likewise, for the Al-maʾmumah (a wound reaching the membrane of the brain), it is also one-third of the full blood money."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5382 - In the narration of Ibn Al-Mughirah from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he said:
"For the baḍiah (a wound that cuts through the flesh after the skin), the compensation is three camels."
records.arabicText: 5382 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "فِي اَلْبَاضِعَةِ ثَلاَثَةٌ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5382 - In the narration of Ibn Al-Mughirah from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "For the baḍiah (a wound that cuts through the flesh after the skin), the compensation is three camels."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5383 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Salih ibn Razin, from Dharih Al-Muharibi, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who was struck by one person with a muḍihah wound (a wound that exposes the bone) and by another person with a damiyah wound (a bleeding wound) in the same instance, and the man died.
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon both of them, divided equally between their wealth."
records.arabicText: 5383 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ رَزِينٍ عَنْ ذَرِيحٍ اَلْمُحَارِبِيِّ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ شَجَّ رَجُلاً مُوضِحَةً وَ شَجَّهُ آخَرُ دَامِيَةً فِي مَقَامٍ وَاحِدٍ فَمَاتَ اَلرَّجُلُ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِمَا اَلدِّيَةُ فِي أَمْوَالِهِمَا نِصْفَيْنِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5383 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Salih ibn Razin, from Dharih Al-Muharibi, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who was struck by one person with a muḍihah wound (a wound that exposes the bone) and by another person with a damiyah wound (a bleeding wound) in the same instance, and the man died.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon both of them, divided equally between their wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5384 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Hayy, who narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about the muḍihah (a wound that exposes the bone) on the head - whether it is the same as on the face.
Imam (as) said: "The muḍihah and other head wounds are the same in blood money (diyyah) whether they occur on the face or the head because the face is part of the head. However, injuries on the body are not treated the same as those on the head."
records.arabicText: 5384 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ حَيٍّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُوضِحَةِ فِي اَلرَّأْسِ كَمَا هِيَ فِي اَلْوَجْهِ فَقَالَ "اَلْمُوضِحَةُ وَ اَلشِّجَاجُ فِي اَلْوَجْهِ وَ اَلرَّأْسِ سَوَاءٌ فِي اَلدِّيَةِ لِأَنَّ اَلْوَجْهَ مِنَ اَلرَّأْسِ وَ لَيْسَ اَلْجِرَاحَاتُ فِي اَلْجَسَدِ كَمَا هِيَ فِي اَلرَّأْسِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5384 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Hasan ibn Hayy, who narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about the muḍihah (a wound that exposes the bone) on the head - whether it is the same as on the face.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "The muḍihah and other head wounds are the same in blood money (diyyah) whether they occur on the face or the head because the face is part of the head. However, injuries on the body are not treated the same as those on the head."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5385 - In the narration of Aban, he said:
"Al-jaifah (a wound that penetrates into the internal cavity) has no qisas (retaliation); only hukumah (compensatory assessment) is applied.
Al-munqilah (a wound that causes bone fragments to dislodge) also has no qisas; only hukumah is applied.
For Al-ma'mumah (a wound that reaches the covering membrane of the brain), one-third of the diyah (blood money) is due, and there is no qisas for it, except for hukumah."
records.arabicText: 5385 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ أَبَانٍ قَالَ: "اَلْجَائِفَةُ مَا وَقَعَتْ فِي اَلْجَوْفِ لَيْسَ لِصَاحِبِهِ قِصَاصٌ إِلاَّ اَلْحُكُومَةُ وَ اَلْمُنَقِّلَةُ تُنَقَّلُ مِنْهَا اَلْعِظَامُ لَيْسَ فِيهَا قِصَاصٌ إِلاَّ اَلْحُكُومَةُ وَ فِي اَلْمَأْمُومَةِ ثُلُثُ اَلدِّيَةِ لَيْسَ فِيهَا قِصَاصٌ إِلاَّ اَلْحُكُومَةُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5385 - In the narration of Aban, he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Al-jaifah (a wound that penetrates into the internal cavity) has no qisas (retaliation); only hukumah (compensatory assessment) is applied.
Al-munqilah (a wound that causes bone fragments to dislodge) also has no qisas; only hukumah is applied.
For Al-ma'mumah (a wound that reaches the covering membrane of the brain), one-third of the diyah (blood money) is due, and there is no qisas for it, except for hukumah."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5386 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni: "Indeed, the Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled that for the hashimah (a wound that fractures the bone), the compensation is ten camels."
records.arabicText: 5386 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى فِي اَلْهَاشِمَةِ بِعَشْرٍ مِنَ اَلْإِبِلِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for Wounds and Head Injuries
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5387 - Abu Abdullah (as) said regarding a slave who struck one man with a muḍihah wound (a wound that exposes the bone) and then struck another man similarly:
"The compensation is to be divided between them."
records.arabicText: 5387 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي عَبْدٍ شَجَّ رَجُلاً مُوضِحَةً ثُمَّ شَجَّ آخَرَ فَقَالَ "هُوَ بَيْنَهُمَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5387 - Abu Abdullah (as) said regarding a slave who struck one man with a muḍihah wound (a wound that exposes the bone) and then struck another man similarly:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The compensation is to be divided between them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5388 - Amr ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Jamilah, from Sa'd Al-Iskaf, from Al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah, who said:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), ruled in a case where a slave girl was riding on another slave girl. A third slave girl prodded the one being ridden, causing her to buck, which led to the rider falling and dying.
He ruled that the diyah (blood money) for the deceased should be divided equally between the one who prodded (nakhisah) and the one who was prodded (mankhusah).
records.arabicText: 5388 - رَوَى عَمْرُو بْنُ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنْ سَعْدٍ اَلْإِسْكَافِ عَنِ اَلْأَصْبَغِ بْنِ نُبَاتَةَ قَالَ: قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي جَارِيَةٍ رَكِبَتْ جَارِيَةً فَنَخَسَتْهَا جَارِيَةٌ أُخْرَى فَقَمَصَتِ اَلْمَرْكُوبَةُ فَصَرَعَتِ اَلرَّاكِبَةَ فَمَاتَتْ فَقَضَى بِدِيَتِهَا نِصْفَيْنِ بَيْنَ اَلنَّاخِسَةِ وَ اَلْمَنْخُوسَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5388 - Amr ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Jamilah, from Sa'd Al-Iskaf, from Al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), ruled in a case where a slave girl was riding on another slave girl. A third slave girl prodded the one being ridden, causing her to buck, which led to the rider falling and dying.
He ruled that the diyah (blood money) for the deceased should be divided equally between the one who prodded (nakhisah) and the one who was prodded (mankhusah).

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5389 - It is narrated from Wahb ibn Wahb, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Whoever kills a close relative (hamim) of a people should seek reconciliation with them as much as he is able, for it will lighten his reckoning."
records.arabicText: 5389 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ وَهْبِ بْنِ وَهْبٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ قَتَلَ حَمِيمَ قَوْمٍ فَلْيُصَالِحْهُمْ مَا قَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ فَإِنَّهُ أَخَفُّ لِحِسَابِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5389 - It is narrated from Wahb ibn Wahb, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever kills a close relative (hamim) of a people should seek reconciliation with them as much as he is able, for it will lighten his reckoning."

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5390 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Al-Thumali, from Sa'id ibn Al-Musayyab, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said:
"If a man strikes another man with a whip, Allah (swt) will strike him with a whip of fire."
records.arabicText: 5390 - رَوَى عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الثُّمَالِيِّ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ‌ قَالَ: لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلًا ضَرَبَ رَجُلًا سَوْطاً لَضَرَبَهُ اللَّهُ سَوْطاً مِنَ النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5390 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Al-Thumali, from Sa'id ibn Al-Musayyab, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man strikes another man with a whip, Allah (swt) will strike him with a whip of fire."

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5391 - In the narration of Ibn Faddal from some of his companions from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said:
"The diyah (compensatory blood money) for a (killing) hunting dog is forty dirhams, the diyah for a livestock dog is twenty dirhams, and the diyah for a dog that is neither for hunting nor for livestock is a basket of dirt - the killer must give it, and the owner must accept it."
records.arabicText: 5391 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "دِيَةُ كَلْبِ اَلصَّيْدِ أَرْبَعُونَ دِرْهَماً وَ دِيَةُ كَلْبِ اَلْمَاشِيَةِ عِشْرُونَ دِرْهَماً وَ دِيَةُ اَلْكَلْبِ اَلَّذِي لَيْسَ لِلصَّيْدِ وَ لاَ لِلْمَاشِيَةِ زَبِيلٌ مِنْ تُرَابٍ عَلَى اَلْقَاتِلِ أَنْ يُعْطِيَ وَ عَلَى صَاحِبِهِ أَنْ يَقْبَلَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5391 - In the narration of Ibn Faddal from some of his companions from Abu Abdullah (as), that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The diyah (compensatory blood money) for a (killing) hunting dog is forty dirhams, the diyah for a livestock dog is twenty dirhams, and the diyah for a dog that is neither for hunting nor for livestock is a basket of dirt - the killer must give it, and the owner must accept it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5392 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Al-Jarud who said: I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say:
"The mule of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) was never stopped from going anywhere it wanted."
Imam (as) said: "A man from Banu Mudlij came, and the mule had entered his field of reeds. He prepared an arrow and killed it."
Then Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said to him: "By Allah (swt), you will not leave me until you compensate for it."
So he compensated for it with six hundred dirhams.
records.arabicText: 5392 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْجَارُودِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: "كَانَتْ بَغْلَةُ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لاَ يَرُدُّوهَا عَنْ شَيْءٍ وَقَعَتْ فِيهِ " قَالَ "فَأَتَاهَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي مُدْلِجٍ وَ قَدْ وَقَعَتْ فِي قَصَبٍ لَهُ فَفَوَّقَ لَهَا سَهْماً فَقَتَلَهَا" فَقَالَ لَهُ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "وَ اَللَّهِ لاَ تُفَارِقُنِي حَتَّى تَدِيَهَا" قَالَ "فَوَدَاهَا سِتَّمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5392 - Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Al-Jarud who said: I heard Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The mule of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) was never stopped from going anywhere it wanted."
Imam (as) said: "A man from Banu Mudlij came, and the mule had entered his field of reeds. He prepared an arrow and killed it."
Then Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said to him: "By Allah (swt), you will not leave me until you compensate for it."
So he compensated for it with six hundred dirhams.

records.chapter: Chapter on Rare Cases of Blood Money (diyyat)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5393 - Jameel ibn Darraj narrated from some of our companions from one of the two Imams (as) regarding a man who broke another man's hand, and then the man's hand healed.
Imam (as) said: "There is no retaliation (qisas) in this case, but he must pay compensation (Al-arsh)."
records.arabicText:
5393 - وَ رَوَى جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ كَسَرَ يَدَ رَجُلٍ ثُمَّ بَرَأَتْ يَدُ اَلرَّجُلِ فَقَالَ
"لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ فِي هَذَا قِصَاصٌ وَ لَكِنَّهُ يُعْطِي اَلْأَرْشَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5393 - Jameel ibn Darraj narrated from some of our companions from one of the two Imams (as) regarding a man who broke another man's hand, and then the man's hand healed.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no retaliation (qisas) in this case, but he must pay compensation (Al-arsh)."
